content: The OpenClaw ACP agent harness architecture, which lets the core autonomous agent loop outsource tool use and reasoning to heterogeneous external coding agents, can be modified to integrate a maximum entropy Soft Q-Learning objective to rank which external harness to select for a given task step. This replaces greedy or fixed routing of tasks to external agents with a stochastic policy that explicitly maintains diversity over harness selections, enabling the autonomous agent to explore under-explored capabilities of lesser-used external coding models while still converging on high-performance task routines over time.
source_topics: agent harness ACP openclaw autonomous agent framework architecture
